steps.title: Identify the spatial dependence that drives the play
steps.content:
The passage states that the play's refusal of narrative coherence “hinges on” the venue's spatial fragmentation.

The phrase hinges on indicates that the play's core nature depends fundamentally on this particular physical environment—not just as a backdrop, but as the source of its defining feature.

steps.title: Apply the spatial dependence to the answer choices
steps.content:
A production whose core feature depends on a specific spatial layout cannot easily transfer to a different venue without losing that essence.

Choice C captures this: the production's dependence on its environment makes exact reproduction difficult elsewhere.

A and D introduce unsupported elements (earlier venues and a recommended itinerary), while B mistakes the intentional design for audience disappointment.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
